v3 = 4(0.004)/(π × 0.06²) = 1.415 m/s.

Therefore the correct answer is 1.528 m/s, 1.592 m/s, 1.415 m/s.

questions.text:
Use the following information to answer questions 19 to 21.

Water flows through a pipe junction where pipes of different diameters are connected as shown in Figure G. Fluid pressure (p1) at section 1 is 25 kPa. Flow rate at section 1 is 12 litres/sec while flow rate at section 3 is 4 litres/sec. Flow directions and pipe diameters are shown in the figure.

Fluid pressures p2 and p3 at sections 2 and 3 respectively are:
questions.imgAlt: Pipe junction with 10 cm, 8 cm and 6 cm diameter branches and indicated flow directions.
questions.exp:
Neglecting losses and elevation differences across the small junction, Bernoulli gives p/ρg + V²/(2g) = constant between the sections.

Between sections 1 and 2:
p2 = p1 + ρ(V1² - V2²)/2 = 25.0 + [1000(1.528² - 1.592²)/2]/1000 = 24.9 kN/m².

Between sections 1 and 3:
p3 = p1 + ρ(V1² - V3²)/2 = 25.0 + [1000(1.528² - 1.415²)/2]/1000 = 25.17 kN/m².

Therefore the correct answer is 24.9 kN/m² and 25.17 kN/m².

questions.text:
Use the following information to answer questions 19 to 21.

Water flows through a pipe junction where pipes of different diameters are connected as shown in Figure G. Fluid pressure (p1) at section 1 is 25 kPa. Flow rate at section 1 is 12 litres/sec while flow rate at section 3 is 4 litres/sec. Flow directions and pipe diameters are shown in the figure.

Resultant force acting on the pipe junction due to water flow is:
questions.imgAlt: Pipe junction with 10 cm, 8 cm and 6 cm diameter branches and indicated flow directions.
questions.exp:
Use the linear momentum equation for the junction. Take x horizontal to the right and y vertically upward. Section 3 is at 60° to the horizontal.

The momentum flux change is ρ(Q2V2 + Q3V3 - Q1V1) as a vector. Including pressure forces at the three sections and using p1 = 25 kPa, p2 = 24.9 kPa and p3 = 25.17 kPa, the support reaction components required to hold the fluid are approximately Rx = 38.41 kN and Ry = -10.26 kN.

The equal and opposite resultant force exerted by the water on the pipe has magnitude R = √(38.41² + 10.26²) = 39.76 kN.

Therefore the correct answer is 39.76 kN.

questions.text:
Use the following information to answer questions 22 to 24.

Two reservoirs with a water level difference of 8 m are connected by a pipe of diameter 120 mm and length 15 m. Darcy Friction Factor λ for the pipe is 0.005. A centrifugal pump having the characteristics shown in Figure H is installed in the pipe and water is pumped from the lower reservoir to the upper reservoir. The minor losses in the pipe can be neglected.

The flow rate in the pipe is (approximately):
questions.imgAlt: Pump head and efficiency curves plotted against flow rate.
questions.exp:
The system head is the static lift plus pipe friction head:

Hsystem = 8 + λ(L/D)(V²/2g), with V = 4Q/(πD²).

Substituting D = 0.12 m, L = 15 m and λ = 0.005 gives Hsystem = 8 + 249.04Q².

The operating point is the intersection of this system characteristic with the pump characteristic in Figure H. From the graph, the intersection occurs at approximately Q = 0.43 m³/s.

Therefore the correct answer is 0.43 m³/s.

questions.text:
Use the following information to answer questions 22 to 24.

Two reservoirs with a water level difference of 8 m are connected by a pipe of diameter 120 mm and length 15 m. Darcy Friction Factor λ for the pipe is 0.005. A centrifugal pump having the characteristics shown in Figure H is installed in the pipe and water is pumped from the lower reservoir to the upper reservoir. The minor losses in the pipe can be neglected.

The power consumption of the pump is (approximately):
questions.imgAlt: Pump head and efficiency curves plotted against flow rate.
questions.exp:
From Q22, the operating flow rate is Q ≈ 0.43 m³/s. From Figure H at this operating point, the pump head is about 54 m and the efficiency is about 70%.

Input power = ρgQH/η = (1000 × 9.81 × 0.43 × 54)/0.70 = 3.25 × 10⁵ W = 325.4 kW.

The nearest option is 326 kW.

questions.text:
Use the following information to answer questions 22 to 24.

Two reservoirs with a water level difference of 8 m are connected by a pipe of diameter 120 mm and length 15 m. Darcy Friction Factor λ for the pipe is 0.005. A centrifugal pump having the characteristics shown in Figure H is installed in the pipe and water is pumped from the lower reservoir to the upper reservoir. The minor losses in the pipe can be neglected.

If two identical pumps with the above characteristics are connected in parallel, the flow rate in the pipe is:
questions.imgAlt: Pump head and efficiency curves plotted against flow rate.
questions.exp:
For two identical pumps connected in parallel, the head at a given discharge through each pump is the same, while the total discharge is doubled. Thus the combined pump characteristic is obtained by doubling the flow rate of the single-pump curve at the same head.

The system characteristic remains unchanged. The new intersection of the system curve with the parallel-pump characteristic gives Q ≈ 0.52 m³/s.

Therefore the correct answer is 0.52 m³/s.

questions.text:
Among the following(s), which is(are) the correct statement(s) related to hydraulic machinery?

i. Reciprocating pump is an example for positive displacement machines.

ii. Pressure of water is decreased when it flows through the impeller of the centrifugal pump.

iii. A centrifugal pump is an axial flow, rotodynamic pump.

iv. In a pump, energy transfer takes place from the operating parts of the pump to the fluid.
questions.exp:
Statement i is correct because a reciprocating pump traps and displaces a fixed volume of fluid, so it is a positive displacement machine.

Statement ii is incorrect because in a centrifugal pump the impeller transfers energy to the water, increasing its pressure and velocity rather than decreasing the pressure.

Statement iii is incorrect because a centrifugal pump is a radial-flow rotodynamic pump, not an axial-flow pump.

Statement iv is correct because a pump transfers mechanical energy from its operating parts to the fluid.

Therefore the correct combination is i and iv only.
